We're fully aware that parents are trying to do the right thing in this space and do what is safe for their children.

Thank you, Jo.
I'm Anita Waring, I'm a senior adviser in the safety team here at the Ministry.
And yeah, again, just acknowledge the petitioner, there's a lot of information and petition,
and as such, the submissions to the committee do actually cover all the detail behind this issue,
so I don't think I need to go into those again.
I did just want to provide a bit more context behind the recent work around this issue,
our children cycling on the footpath and provide a bit of an update on where we are, where we are now.
So previous work around this issue has always sought to balance the views of many different users and the needs.
of those different users on the footpath. Obviously there's other shared spaces but this petition is
specifically around users of the footpath. So we recognise there is an increasing number of
different users using these places and that the rules have not always kept up to date with
these changes in the use and some advancements in technologies and such.
As noted in our submissions as part of accessible streets, we consulted back in 2020 on allowing
cycling on the footpath. This would have obviously allowed children on the footpath but it also
recognised that some adults also don't feel safe cycling on the road. That proposal received
mixed feedback through public consultation and it really highlighted the views and the concerns
of all those different users. In response to the feedback the Ministry and NZTA undertook
further policy work to make sure that we got the balance right and as a result we then the Ministry
then recommended to the Minister that that proposal be amended to allow children aged 12 and under
to cycle on the footpath and their accompanying caregivers. I just want to clarify in our
written submission we noted that accessible streets allowed children to are consulted on allowing
children to ride on the footpath. This was included as an alternative option but it did
initially propose all age cycling and we then revised our recommendation to allow children
12 and under. The Minister of Transport took this package to Cabinet in early 2023
but it was not progressed. It was deferred and then there was obviously the change of government
in October 2023. So that's a brief history of the previous work and what is happening
currently in October 2024 so the government
leistits road safety objectives.

So, there's work being done in this year. Was there anything that you've heard today just like to add to, to give any, feel free to, I'm just asking the officials if they would like to?
I think the petitioners, Shane and Benedict, have comprehensively covered this issue.
Yeah, Accessible Streets did propose a comprehensive package of rule changes that did propose a speed limit.
It was really trying to factor in all the different users, the elderly, the disability community, who often experience other barriers or limitations in their mobility and moving around.
So, we are certainly aware of all the issues and we have proposed changes before and we have an opportunity now to propose changes again to the new minister under his Road Safety Objectives action.
Just to note, while we don't have anything around the older folk, there was a disability assessment undertaken by NZTA around these proposals as well.
And they did actually find that whilst disabled people do have concerns about people on the footpath,
actually over in all that community also did think it was sensible to have kids on the footpath.
So it might be that we just needed to get into some of those issues a bit further.
Good. All right. Well, thank you.
Thank you.
I had one question.
Is having kids on the footpath specifically part of the work programme going to the Minister?
Because that wasn't totally clear from what you said.
So the Minister has to currently consider his overall wrongs programme.
This proposal is one thing that he is considering, but it's not confirmed at this stage.
So we had to wait for him to take those decisions.
Okay. Thanks.
